title: Nitrilotriacetic acid trisodium salt - Canada.ca
content:
## Nitrilotriacetic acid trisodium salt
- Nitrilotriacetic acid trisodium salt
This substance was identified for action under the Chemicals Management Plan (CMP).
- Summary of publications
- Follow-up activities
Public comments on the draft screening assessment
Final screening assessment
Conclusion on section 64 criteria
Follow-up activities
Nitrilotriacetic acid trisodium salt (Na 3 NTA)
Glycine, N,N-bis(carboxymethyl)-, trisodium salt
Significant New Activity (SNAc) provisions

## Follow-up activities
Although Na 3 NTA is not considered to be harmful to human health at current levels of exposure, this substance is considered to have a health effect of concern based on its potential ability to cause cancer of the urinary tract based on laboratory studies. Therefore, there may be a potential risk if exposure to this substance were to increase. The proposed follow-up activity is to apply the SNAc provisions of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 (CEPA 1999) to Na 3 NTA.
Publication in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 156, No. 48 , of a notice of intent to amend the Domestic Substances List to apply the SNAc provisions of CEPA 1999 and start of a 60-day public comment period.
Publication of the final screening assessment. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 156, No. 48 .
Publication and start of 60-day public comment period on the draft screening assessment. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 154, No. 51 .
